comment: Machine-readable mirror of docs/risk-register.md. Read by scripts/production-readiness.mjs. Open + P0 → red row in the aggregator; open + P1 → yellow; mitigating + P0 → yellow; closed → not surfaced. When this file changes, update the .md mirror in the same PR.

risks.title: External smart-contract audit not yet complete (BrainEscrow)
risks.mitigation_summary: Mainnet boot-fenced (escrow-audit-gate.ts) + CI guard (check-escrow-audit-marker) + 4 Foundry invariants on the contract.
exit_criteria: external audit completes with no critical or high findings
audited commit SHA + tag pinned in contracts/AUDIT-SCOPE.md
deployed mainnet bytecode verified to match audited bytecode
BRAIN_ESCROW_AUDIT_RECEIPT set to the audit report URL/hash (or legacy BRAIN_ESCROW_AUDIT_APPROVED="true") in production env

risks.title: Tenant blob purge not implemented (GDPR Article 17 gap)
risks.mitigation_summary: DELETE /v1/tenants/{id} surfaces blob URIs pending purge; RFC 0003 phase A landed, phase B awaits signoff.
exit_criteria: tenant_blob_purge_jobs migration
BlobAdapter.purge() on memory/s3/azure
tenant-blob-purge-worker.ts
check-blob-purge-callsites.mjs lint guard
integration test proving end-to-end erasure

risks.title: Azure production deploy chain not yet exercised
risks.mitigation_summary: All safety controls (boot fences, gate, RLS) are deploy-substrate-independent. Disclosure explicit in architecture/enterprise-readiness.md.
exit_criteria: Azure OIDC secrets provisioned
build push deploy staging-validation chain runs cleanly end-to-end
production deploy logs show all 5 boot fences armed via brain.runtime.capabilities
rollback procedure exercised against staging

risks.title: Full money-movement E2E not yet enforced in CI
risks.mitigation_summary: Strict-mode demo (BRAIN_DEMO_STRICT_PROOF) runs the full chain locally; e2e probe asserts gate dormancy from staging history. CI-enforced full E2E pending deterministic stack-in-CI.
exit_criteria: deterministic local-stack-in-CI (pg+redis+localstack+boot binary in GH Actions)
golden-path demo runs in strict mode on every PR
any §6 check 8 / 9.5 / 11.5 going not_applicable fails the build
audit-before → audit-after linkage asserted

risks.title: Rail catalog vs docs drift
risks.mitigation_summary: check-rails-catalog-drift CI guard wired into pnpm run lint compares both files; drift is now structurally impossible at PR merge time.

risks.title: BrainSmartAccount session-key caps are unit-blind to ERC20 decimals
risks.mitigation_summary: Caps (maxPerTx / maxPerPeriod) are uint256 with no token-unit context. Comment says wei (18dp) but executor decodes raw ERC20 amounts (USDC=6dp) and compares directly. Sepolia-only, no mainnet exposure; external audit (R-01) would surface this, but fixing pre-audit is cheaper. Flagged by Opus 4.8 peer review at commit 1985f68 (finding F-3).
exit_criteria: SessionKey struct carries an explicit token + decimals (or a canonical wei-denomination invariant)
executeViaSessionKey verifies the call target equals the configured token when value=0
Foundry tests cover a 6-decimal USDC cap AND an 18-decimal ERC20 cap, proving caps enforce in the token's native units
Audit scope updated: contracts/AUDIT-SCOPE.md notes the fix

risks.title: BrainSmartAccount selector decode covers only three ERC20 selectors; non-decodable selectors bypass caps
risks.mitigation_summary: Only transfer/approve/transferFrom selectors are decoded; any other allowlisted selector leaves capAmount=0 and passes every cap unmetered. Mitigated today because grantSessionKey is gated by the operator and the allowlist is opt-in. But the contract should refuse to grant a session key with non-decodable selectors, not silently disable metering. Flagged by Opus 4.8 peer review at commit 1985f68 (finding F-4).
exit_criteria: grantSessionKey() rejects any selector outside the decodable set, OR adds an explicit per-selector decoder + cap-enforcement path
Foundry test proves a grant containing a non-decodable selector reverts
Foundry test proves a granted key cannot bypass maxPerTx via a non-decodable call
Audit scope updated: contracts/AUDIT-SCOPE.md notes the fix

risks.title: Money-touching rail-adapter test coverage gap
risks.mitigation_summary: Batch 8 P5: error-path coverage added to onchainExecutor (read/write error propagation, receipt-hash sourcing), plaidClient (Plaid API error verbatim), and x402Client (full lifecycle: decimals read, transfer revert, receipt timeout, facilitator 5xx + network error, idempotency-key forwarding, chain selection). PostgresSourceRepository was already at 16-test coverage (reviewer's claim of near-zero was stale). 23 rail-adapter unit tests now pass (was 6).
exit_criteria: Coverage parity with the §6 gate measured at >=80% line coverage on the adapter files (next step: wire coverage gates into test:coverage)
Integration tests on testnet for at least the onchain executor end-to-end

risks.title: On-chain tumbling vs off-chain rolling spend windows can diverge
risks.mitigation_summary: Batch 8 P3: off-chain agent-window-spend loader switched from a trailing rolling window to a tumbling window using the same floor((ts/period)*period) formula as BrainSmartAccount._windowSpent. Off-chain (agent-budget, check 8.5) and on-chain (session-key budget) now agree at boundaries. The misleading 'mirror' comment in shared/src/gate/gate.ts was corrected. Approve-as-spend concern is structurally NA off-chain (only executed intents are summed; approve never reaches executed).
exit_criteria: Property tests proving off-chain SQL window matches on-chain Solidity window at boundary T = floor(now/period)*period and T-1, T+1
Optional: rationalize approve-as-spend on-chain (defer; current is conservative)

risks.title: BrainSmartAccount NatSpec framing claims 'ERC-4337-style' but no EntryPoint exists
risks.mitigation_summary: BrainSmartAccount.sol contract NatSpec rewritten: now describes the actual design ('Smart account with directly-called session keys. Not ERC-4337: there is no EntryPoint, no UserOperation, no paymaster'). AUDIT-SCOPE.md mirror updated. Surfaced by Opus 4.8 (F-11).
